description: Lifecycle management of OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect clients — admin CRUD plus self-service OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591).

actors.name: Admin API Consumer
actors.description: Trusted backend operator with full CRUD access via privileged admin endpoints. Can set admin-only fields: metadata, access_token_strategy, skip_consent, skip_logout_consent.

actors.name: Client Owner
actors.description: Application developer or automated service that self-registers and manages its own OAuth 2.0 client via the public OIDC Dynamic Client Registration endpoint using a registration_access_token.

validation.message: client_secret must be at least 6 characters long.

fields.name: refresh_token_grant_access_token_lifespan
fields.label: Refresh Token Grant Access Token Lifespan

fields.name: refresh_token_grant_id_token_lifespan
fields.label: Refresh Token Grant ID Token Lifespan

fields.name: refresh_token_grant_refresh_token_lifespan
fields.label: Refresh Token Grant Refresh Token Lifespan

authentication.admin_api: All /admin/clients/* endpoints are privileged — must be protected by network policy, gateway auth, or service mesh. No built-in auth in handler.
authentication.dynamic_registration: /oauth2/register/{id} GET/PUT/DELETE require a valid registration_access_token (Bearer). Validated via HMAC strategy; constant-time compared against stored signature to prevent timing attacks. Source: client/handler.go ValidDynamicAuth()
authentication.registration_token_rotation: On every successful PUT to /oauth2/register/{id}, a new registration_access_token is generated and the old one invalidated. Source: client/handler.go setOidcDynamicClient()

input_validation.secret_minimum_length: client_secret must be >= 6 chars. Auto-generated secrets are 26 chars. Source: client/validator.go:87
input_validation.redirect_uri_fragments: Redirect URIs must not contain fragments (#). Source: client/validator.go — strings.Contains(r, "#")
input_validation.jwks_mutual_exclusion: jwks and jwks_uri cannot both be set. Source: client/validator.go:60
input_validation.private_key_jwt_requirements: When token_endpoint_auth_method=private_key_jwt, either jwks or jwks_uri must be set. token_endpoint_auth_signing_alg (if set) must be one of: RS256/RS384/RS512/PS256/PS384/PS512/ES256/ES384/ES512. Source: client/validator.go:53-58
input_validation.uri_scheme_validation: policy_uri, tos_uri, logo_uri, client_uri must be valid http or https URLs. Source: client/validator.go — URI scheme check loop
input_validation.cors_origin_format: allowed_cors_origins must be scheme://host[:port] only — no path, query, fragment, or embedded credentials. Source: client/validator.go — CORS validation loop
input_validation.post_logout_redirect_uri_match: Each post_logout_redirect_uri must share scheme, hostname, and port with at least one registered redirect_uri. Source: client/validator.go — ContainsFunc check
input_validation.sector_identifier_uri_validation: sector_identifier_uri must be HTTPS. Server fetches it at registration and validates all redirect_uris appear in the returned JSON array. Source: client/validator.go ValidateSectorIdentifierURL()
input_validation.private_ip_restriction: When ClientHTTPNoPrivateIPRanges=true (configurable), jwks_uri, backchannel_logout_uri, and request_uris must not resolve to private IP ranges. Source: client/validator.go — ipx.AreAllAssociatedIPsAllowed()
input_validation.subject_type_validation: subject_type must be in server's configured subject_types_supported. Defaults to public if supported. Source: client/validator.go
input_validation.userinfo_alg_validation: userinfo_signed_response_alg must be none or RS256. Source: client/validator.go:100

secret_handling.never_return_in_list_or_get: client_secret is always stripped from GET /admin/clients and GET /admin/clients/{id} responses. Source: client/handler.go — c[k].Secret = "" and c.Secret = ""
secret_handling.secret_expires_at_always_zero: client_secret_expires_at is always set to 0 (expiry not supported). Source: client/validator.go — c.SecretExpiresAt = 0
secret_handling.only_echoed_on_create_update: Secret is only returned in the response body of create and update operations for non-public clients. Never persisted in plaintext.

access.dynamic_registration_disabled_by_default: /oauth2/register endpoints are disabled by default. Enabled via: oidc.dynamic_client_registration.enabled=true. Source: client/handler.go requireDynamicAuth() + driver/config/provider.go KeyPublicAllowDynamicRegistration
access.dynamic_registration_field_restrictions: Dynamic Client Registration cannot set: client_secret, client_id, metadata, access_token_strategy, skip_consent, skip_logout_consent. Source: client/handler.go createOidcDynamicClient() + ValidateDynamicRegistration()
access.dynamic_get_excludes_metadata: GET /oauth2/register/{id} strips both Secret and Metadata from the response. Source: client/handler.go getOidcDynamicClient() — c.Metadata = nil

any: registration_access_token missing from Authorization header
registration_access_token is malformed or expired
token signature does not match stored signature
client has no registration access token

dynamic_registration_unauthorized.result: 401 Unauthorized. Generic message to prevent client ID enumeration.

any: a redirect_uri contains a fragment (#)
a redirect_uri is not a valid URL
dynamic registration request includes client_secret
dynamic registration request includes metadata
dynamic registration request sets skip_consent=true
dynamic registration request sets skip_logout_consent=true
dynamic registration request sets access_token_strategy
private_key_jwt auth without jwks or jwks_uri
both jwks and jwks_uri are set
a JWK in the inline jwks set is invalid
a URI field is not a valid http/https URL
client_secret is shorter than 6 characters
userinfo_signed_response_alg is not none or RS256
subject_type is not in server's supported list
allowed_cors_origins entry has a path, query, or credentials
post_logout_redirect_uri does not match any redirect_uri domain/port/scheme
sector_identifier_uri is not HTTPS or missing redirect_uris in its array
jwks_uri, backchannel_logout_uri, or request_uri resolves to private IP range
access_token_strategy is not jwt or opaque

given: no client exists with the requested client_id
admin API caller provides valid client configuration
client_created.result: 201 Created. Full client object returned including client_secret (plaintext, once only) and registration_access_token. Secret is hashed in storage and never retrievable again.

clients_listed.result: 200 OK. Array of client objects, paginated via Link header (keyset pagination). client_secret stripped from all records. Default page size: 100.

client_retrieved.result: 200 OK. Full client record. client_secret always omitted.

given: request body passes all validation rules
client_replaced.result: 200 OK. Updated client. client_secret echoed if provided in request; existing secret preserved otherwise.

given: patch does not attempt to modify the client_id field
client_patched.result: 200 OK. Patched client. client_secret echoed if patch modified it.

given: lifespan values are valid duration strings
client_lifespans_updated.result: 200 OK. Client returned with updated lifespan values. Only lifespan fields are modified; all other client fields are preserved.

given: request does not include any admin-only fields
client_registered_dynamic.result: 201 Created. Server-assigned client_id and client_secret returned (once only). registration_access_token issued for self-managing this client.

given: token signature matches stored signature (constant-time compare)
dynamic_client_self_managed.result: Client owner can GET (200), PUT (200, token rotated), or DELETE (204) their registration. GET strips client_secret and metadata. PUT issues new registration_access_token.

errors.message: The value of one of the Client Metadata fields is invalid and the server has rejected this request.
The value of one or more redirect_uris is invalid.
The request includes an unsupported parameter value or is otherwise malformed.
The requested OAuth 2.0 client does not exist.
The requested OAuth 2.0 client does not exist or you provided incorrect credentials.
Dynamic client registration is not enabled.

events.name: client.created
events.description: Fired when a new OAuth 2.0 client is registered via the admin API.

events.name: client.registered
events.description: Fired when a client self-registers via OIDC Dynamic Client Registration.

events.name: client.deleted
events.description: Fired when an OAuth 2.0 client is deleted.

related.reason: Authorization server that issues tokens using these client registrations.
Social login flows consume OAuth 2.0 clients configured here.
Governs token lifespans, supported subject types, and default scopes referenced by client registrations.
Consent screen logic triggered during authorization for clients where skip_consent is false.

goals.description: Lifecycle management of OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect clients — admin CRUD plus self-service OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591).

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Errors that auto-recover without manual intervention

constraints.description: Must degrade gracefully when dependencies are unavailable
Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

tradeoffs.reason: integration failures can cascade across systems

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details

tech_stack.multi_tenancy: NID-scoped — all client records namespaced per network

public_dynamic_registration: GET    /oauth2/register/{id}         — get own registration (secret + metadata stripped)

goals.description: Full lifecycle management of OAuth 2.0 / OIDC clients — admin CRUD and self-service Dynamic Client Registration with security invariants enforced.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Errors that auto-recover without manual intervention

constraints.description: Must degrade gracefully when the database is unavailable.
client_secret must never appear in logs, list responses, or GET responses. registration_access_token must be rotated on every DCR update.

tradeoffs.reason: Secrets are never retrievable after creation — intentional trade-off to prevent credential leakage.
